records.title: Definition of State
records.description: In this Part, unless the context otherwise requires, 'the State' includes the Government and Parliament of India and the Government and the Legislature of each of the States and all local or other authorities within the territory of India or under the control of the Government of India.
records.keywords: government, definition, part, unless, context, otherwise, requires, includes, parliament, legislature, each, states

records.title: Laws inconsistent with or in derogation of the fundamental rights
records.description: All laws in force in the territory of India immediately before the commencement of this Constitution, in so far as they are inconsistent with the provisions of this Part, shall, to the extent of such inconsistency, be void.
records.keywords: laws, inconsistent, derogation, fundamental, rights, force, territory, immediately, before, commencement, constitution, provisions

records.title: Equality before law
records.description: The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.
records.keywords: equality, before, deny, equal, protection, laws, within, territory

records.title: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
records.description: The State shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth or any of them.
records.keywords: grounds, religion, race, caste, place, birth, prohibition, discrimination, discriminate, against, citizen, only

records.title: Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment
records.description: There shall be equality of opportunity for all citizens in matters relating to employment or appointment to any office under the State.
records.keywords: equality, opportunity, matters, employment, public, citizens, relating, appointment, office

records.title: Abolition of Untouchability
records.description: Untouchability is abolished and its practice in any form is forbidden. The enforcement of any disability arising out of Untouchability shall be an offence punishable in accordance with law.
records.keywords: untouchability, abolition, abolished, practice, form, forbidden, enforcement, disability, arising, offence, punishable, accordance

records.title: Abolition of titles
records.description: No title, not being a military or academic distinction, shall be conferred by the State.
records.keywords: abolition, titles, title, military, academic, distinction, conferred

records.title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech
records.description: All citizens shall have the right to freedom of speech and expression; to assemble peaceably and without arms; to form associations or unions; to move freely throughout the territory of India; to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India; and to practise any profession, or to carry on any occupation, trade or business.
records.keywords: freedom, speech, territory, protection, certain, rights, regarding, citizens, expression, assemble, peaceably, without

records.title: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
records.description: No person shall be convicted of any offence except for violation of a law in force at the time of the commission of the act charged as an offence, nor be subjected to a penalty greater than that which might have been inflicted under the law in force at the time of the commission of the offence.
records.keywords: offence, force, time, commission, protection, respect, conviction, offences, convicted, except, violation, charged

records.title: Protection of life and personal liberty
records.description: No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.
records.keywords: life, personal, liberty, protection, deprived, except, according, procedure, established

records.title: Right to Education
records.description: The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years in such manner as the State may, by law, determine.
records.keywords: education, provide, free, compulsory, children, fourteen, years, manner, determine

records.title: Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases
records.description: No person who is arrested shall be detained in custody without being informed, as soon as may be, of the grounds for such arrest nor shall he be denied the right to consult, and to be defended by, a legal practitioner of his choice.
records.keywords: arrest, protection, against, detention, certain, cases, arrested, detained, custody, without, informed, soon

records.title: Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour
records.description: Traffic in human beings and begar and other similar forms of forced labour are prohibited and any contravention of this provision shall be an offence punishable in accordance with law.
records.keywords: traffic, human, beings, forced, labour, prohibition, begar, other, similar, forms, prohibited, contravention

records.title: Prohibition of employment of children in factories
records.description: No child below the age of fourteen years shall be employed to work in any factory or mine or engaged in any other hazardous employment.
records.keywords: employment, prohibition, children, factories, child, below, fourteen, years, employed, work, factory, mine

records.title: Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion
records.description: Subject to public order, morality and health and to the other provisions of this Part, all persons are equally entitled to freedom of conscience and the right freely to profess, practise and propagate religion.
records.keywords: freedom, conscience, religion, free, profession, practice, propagation, subject, public, order, morality, health

records.title: Remedies for enforcement of rights conferred by this Part
records.description: The right to move the Supreme Court by appropriate proceedings for the enforcement of the rights conferred by this Part is guaranteed.
records.keywords: enforcement, rights, conferred, part, remedies, move, supreme, court, appropriate, proceedings, guaranteed

records.title: Application of the principles contained in this Part
records.description: The provisions contained in this Part shall not be enforceable by any court, but the principles therein laid down are nevertheless fundamental in the governance of the country and it shall be the duty of the State to apply these principles in making laws.
records.keywords: principles, contained, part, application, provisions, enforceable, court, therein, laid, down, nevertheless, fundamental
records.part: Part IV — Directive Principles of State Policy

records.title: Equal justice and free legal aid
records.description: The State shall secure that the operation of the legal system promotes justice, on a basis of equal opportunity, and shall, in particular, provide free legal aid, by suitable legislation or schemes or in any other way, to ensure that opportunities for securing justice are not denied to any citizen by reason of economic or other disabilities.
records.keywords: justice, legal, equal, free, other, secure, operation, system, promotes, basis, opportunity, particular
records.part: Part IV — Directive Principles of State Policy

records.title: Fundamental Duties
records.description: It shall be the duty of every citizen of India to abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals and institutions, the National Flag and the National Anthem; to cherish and follow the noble ideals which inspired our national struggle for freedom; to uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India; to defend the country and render national service when called upon to do so.
records.keywords: national, ideals, fundamental, duties, duty, citizen, abide, constitution, respect, institutions, flag, anthem

records.title: Power of President to grant pardons
records.description: The President shall have the power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remissions of punishment or to suspend, remit or commute the sentence of any person convicted of any offence.
records.keywords: power, president, grant, pardons, reprieves, respites, remissions, punishment, suspend, remit, commute, sentence

records.title: Establishment and constitution of Supreme Court
records.description: There shall be a Supreme Court of India consisting of a Chief Justice of India and, until Parliament by law prescribes a larger number, of not more than thirty-three other Judges.
records.keywords: supreme, court, establishment, constitution, consisting, chief, justice, until, parliament, prescribes, larger, number

records.title: Special leave to appeal by the Supreme Court
records.description: Notwithstanding anything in this Chapter, the Supreme Court may, in its discretion, grant special leave to appeal from any judgment, decree, determination, sentence or order in any cause or matter passed or made by any court or tribunal in the territory of India.
records.keywords: court, special, leave, appeal, supreme, notwithstanding, anything, chapter, discretion, grant, judgment, decree

records.title: Law declared by Supreme Court to be binding on all courts
records.description: The law declared by the Supreme Court shall be binding on all courts within the territory of India.
records.keywords: declared, supreme, court, binding, courts, within, territory

records.title: Enforcement of decrees and orders of Supreme Court
records.description: The Supreme Court in the exercise of its jurisdiction may pass such decree or make such order as is necessary for doing complete justice in any cause or matter pending before it.
records.keywords: supreme, court, enforcement, decrees, orders, exercise, jurisdiction, pass, decree, make, order, necessary

records.title: Power of High Courts to issue certain writs
records.description: Notwithstanding anything in article 32, every High Court shall have power, throughout the territories in relation to which it exercises jurisdiction, to issue to any person or authority, including in appropriate cases, any Government, within those territories directions, orders or writs, including writs in the nature of habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari.
records.keywords: writs, power, high, issue, territories, including, courts, certain, notwithstanding, anything, court, throughout

records.title: Persons not to be deprived of property save by authority of law
records.description: No person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law.
records.keywords: deprived, property, save, authority, persons
records.part: Part XII — Finance, Property, Contracts and Suits

records.title: Superintendence, direction and control of elections
records.description: The superintendence, direction and control of the preparation of the electoral rolls for, and the conduct of, all elections to Parliament and to the Legislature of every State and of elections to the offices of President and Vice-President shall be vested in the Election Commission.
records.keywords: elections, superintendence, direction, control, president, preparation, electoral, rolls, conduct, parliament, legislature, offices

records.title: Proclamation of Emergency
records.description: If the President is satisfied that a grave emergency exists whereby the security of India or of any part of the territory thereof is threatened, whether by war or external aggression or armed rebellion, he may, by Proclamation, make a declaration to that effect in respect of the whole of India or of such part of the territory thereof as may be specified in the Proclamation.
records.keywords: proclamation, emergency, part, territory, thereof, president, satisfied, grave, exists, whereby, security, threatened

records.title: Provisions in case of failure of constitutional machinery in States
records.description: If the President, on receipt of report from the Governor of the State or otherwise, is satisfied that the government of the State cannot be carried on in accordance with the provisions of this Constitution, the President may by Proclamation assume to himself all or any of the functions of the Government of the State.
records.keywords: provisions, president, government, case, failure, constitutional, machinery, states, receipt, report, governor, otherwise

records.title: Power of Parliament to amend the Constitution and procedure therefor
records.description: Notwithstanding anything in this Constitution, Parliament may in exercise of its constituent power amend by way of addition, variation or repeal any provision of this Constitution in accordance with the procedure laid down in this article.
records.keywords: constitution, power, parliament, amend, procedure, therefor, notwithstanding, anything, exercise, constituent, addition, variation
records.part: Part XX — Amendment of the Constitution

records.title: First Amendment (1951)
records.description: Added restrictions on freedom of speech and expression in Article 19, added the Ninth Schedule protecting certain land reform laws from judicial review.
records.keywords: added, first, amendment, restrictions, freedom, speech, expression, ninth, schedule, protecting, certain, land

records.title: Forty-second Amendment (Mini-Constitution) (1976)
records.description: Added 'socialist', 'secular', and 'integrity' to the Preamble. Added Fundamental Duties (Article 51A). Made Directive Principles supersede Fundamental Rights.
records.keywords: added, fundamental, forty, second, amendment, mini, constitution, socialist, secular, integrity, preamble, duties

records.title: Forty-fourth Amendment (1978)
records.description: Removed the right to property from Fundamental Rights (Article 19(1)(f) deleted). Added Article 300A — property can only be taken by authority of law.
records.keywords: property, forty, fourth, amendment, removed, fundamental, rights, deleted, added, only, taken, authority

records.title: Seventy-third Amendment — Panchayati Raj (1992)
records.description: Added Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule to give constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions and provide for reservation of seats for SC/ST and women.
records.keywords: panchayati, seventy, third, amendment, added, part, eleventh, schedule, give, constitutional, status, institutions

records.title: Eighty-sixth Amendment — Right to Education (2002)
records.description: Inserted Article 21A making education a fundamental right for children aged 6–14. Amended Article 45 and Article 51A(k) accordingly.
records.keywords: education, eighty, sixth, amendment, inserted, making, fundamental, children, aged, amended, accordingly

records.title: Hundred and First Amendment — GST (2016)
records.description: Introduced Goods and Services Tax (GST) replacing multiple indirect taxes. Amended Articles 246A, 269A, 279A and inserted the new GST Council.
records.keywords: hundred, first, amendment, introduced, goods, services, replacing, multiple, indirect, taxes, amended, articles

records.title: Hundred and Third Amendment — EWS Reservation (2019)
records.description: Provided for 10% reservation in educational institutions and public employment for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) not covered by existing reservations.
records.keywords: reservation, hundred, third, amendment, educational, institutions, public, employment, economically, weaker, sections, covered

records.title: Hundred and Sixth Amendment — Women's Reservation (2023)
records.description: Reserved one-third of seats in the House of the People and State Assemblies for women (Articles 330A and 332A inserted).
records.keywords: women, hundred, sixth, amendment, reservation, reserved, third, seats, house, people, assemblies, articles
